description_wiki: Kumamoto Prefecture (熊本県, Kumamoto-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [kɯ.ma.mo.to, -toꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Kyūshū.

description: county seat of San Luis Obispo County, California, United States
1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: detainee camp in Arizona, United States
description_wiki:
The Poston Internment Camp, located in Yuma County (now in La Paz County) in southwestern Arizona, was the largest (in terms of area) of the 10 American concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority during World War II.
The site was composed of three separate camps arranged in a chain from north to south, three miles from each other.

description: 1942 U.S. presidential order for the internment of Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: Executive Order 9066 was a United States presidential executive order signed and issued during World War II by United States president Franklin D. Roosevelt on February 19, 1942.

description: city in and county seat of Cuyahoga County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Cleveland ( KLEEV-lənd) is a city in the U.S. state of Ohio and the county seat of Cuyahoga County.

aliases: The Biggest Small Town in America
description: capital city of Ohio, United States of America
description_wiki: Columbus ( kə-LUM-bəs) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Ohio.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

Kibei (kibei (帰米; literally "go home to America")) refers to Japanese Americans born in the United States whose parents had sent them to receive their education in Japan and who had then returned to the US by the time that the US and Japan became engaged in war in the 1940s.

description: public research university in Cincinnati, Ohio, USA
description_wiki: The University of Cincinnati (UC or Cincinnati, informally Cincy) is a public research university in Cincinnati, Ohio, United States.

description: public university in Sacramento, California, USA
description_wiki: California State University, Sacramento (CSUS, Sacramento State, or informally Sac State) is a public university in Sacramento, California, United States.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Newark ( NEW-ərk) is a city in Alameda County, California, United States.

description: infantry regiment of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 442nd Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description_wiki: India, officially the Republic of India, is a country in South Asia.
